main_ingredients: butter or ghee (for brushing after baking)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of plain naan is 1 piece (approximately 120g) — a large, tear-drop shaped, soft and slightly chewy flatbread with golden-brown blisters from the tandoor, brushed with butter or ghee after baking.

nutrient_source.title: Plain Naan — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05030007; cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in HPB Foodo under food code F05030007 — Plain Naan, standard tandoor-baked preparation.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — white flour naan GI approximately 71; refined flour and minimal fibre give a high GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 582mg per piece (120g) — elevated; naan contains yogurt, salt in dough, and butter brushing, all contributing to high sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit naan to half a piece or choose chapati instead. Naan has more than twice the sodium of chapati at equivalent serving sizes.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~71) from refined flour; glycaemic load per full piece (120g) is approximately 42 — high. Minimal fibre provides no protection against blood glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit to half a piece or choose chapati (whole wheat) instead, which has a significantly lower GI (~52). Pair with high-protein curries to moderate the glycaemic response.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined flour is neutral for inflammation at standard serving levels; butter provides small amounts of fat-soluble vitamins.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable in moderation for patients with rheumatoid arthritis. No specific anti-inflammatory benefit from plain naan. Whole wheat alternatives (chapati, roti) are nutritionally superior.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain Naan is a leavened flatbread — synonymous with North Indian restaurant dining — made from refined white flour (maida) leavened with yogurt and yeast or baking powder, stretched by hand into a teardrop shape and baked at high heat directly on the inner wall of a clay tandoor oven. The surface develops beautiful golden-brown blisters while the interior remains soft and chewy. After baking, it is brushed with butter or ghee. Compared to chapati, naan is higher in calories, fat, sodium, and has a higher GI due to the refined flour, but has a superior soft texture that makes it a popular restaurant choice.
common_user_queries: naan vs chapati which is healthier

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code F05030007 — Plain Naan, standard preparation.
notes: Contains gluten (refined wheat) and dairy (yogurt, butter). Not vegan or suitable for gluten-sensitive patients. Cholesterol approximately 14mg per piece (120g) from butter/yogurt. Low fibre (1.8g/100g) compared to chapati (5.5g/100g). Garlic naan and butter naan have higher fat and calorie content.
